description: Figure 6 presents transmission electron microscopy (TEM) images of Fe-Mn-Al-C-(Ni) based austenitic low-density steels after cold rolling and subsequent heat treatment at 973 K, specifically comparing 28Mn0Ni (a-d), 28Mn5Ni (e-f), and 15Mn5Ni (g-h) alloys. The set comprises bright-field (BF) images (a, c, e, g) and dark-field (DF) images (b, d, f, h) captured using the (010)κ satellite spot corresponding to κ-carbides, as indicated by selected area electron diffraction patterns (SAED/SADP) insets in (a-d) showing the [101] zone axis with marked 010κ and 101κ reflections. The images reveal the precipitation morphology and distribution of κ-carbides (marked by dotted orange arrows) and B2 intermetallic particles (intergranular indicated by broken yellow arrows and intragranular by solid white arrows). In the Ni-free 28Mn0Ni alloy, fine uniformly distributed intragranular κ-carbides are observed within recrystallized austenite regions (b), whereas deformed austenite regions contain inhomogeneous, relatively coarse κ-carbides of 10-60 nm (d). For Ni-containing alloys (28Mn5Ni and 15Mn5Ni), both fine intragranular B2 particles (5-20 nm) and coarser intergranular B2 particles (50-200 nm) are visible alongside κ-carbides (e-h). The DF images specifically highlight κ-carbide distributions, showing higher density and coarser sizes in deformed austenite regions compared to recrystallized areas. These observations support that Ni addition promotes B2 precipitation and that highly strained regions serve as preferential nucleation sites for both κ-carbides and B2 particles, critically influencing the microstructural evolution and mechanical property balance in these low-density steels.
